constraints.title: Include kind field
constraints.rule: All JSON objects returned by an API include a `kind` field.
constraints.rationale: Direct decomposition of the required `kind` field.

constraints.title: Include apiVersion field
constraints.rule: All JSON objects returned by an API include an `apiVersion` field.
constraints.rationale: Direct decomposition of the required `apiVersion` field.

constraints.title: Include metadata.namespace
constraints.rule: Every object kind includes `metadata.namespace`.
constraints.rationale: Direct decomposition of the required namespace metadata field.

constraints.title: Include metadata.name
constraints.rule: Every object kind includes `metadata.name`.
constraints.rationale: Direct decomposition of the required name metadata field.

constraints.title: Include metadata.uid
constraints.rule: Every object kind includes `metadata.uid`.
constraints.rationale: Direct decomposition of the required uid metadata field.

constraints.title: Treat resourceVersion as opaque
constraints.rule: Clients treat `metadata.resourceVersion` as opaque and pass it back unmodified.
constraints.rationale: The core actionable part of the `resourceVersion` rule is opacity and round-trip preservation.

constraints.title: Use declarative spec fields
constraints.rule: Fields in `spec` use declarative rather than imperative names and semantics.
constraints.rationale: This is a central semantic API-design rule.

constraints.title: Ignore status on PUT and POST
constraints.rule: PUT and POST on objects ignore `status` values.
constraints.rationale: This is an explicit update-semantics rule.

constraints.title: Provide status subresource
constraints.rule: Resources managed through status updates provide a `/status` subresource.
constraints.rationale: This is the explicit mechanism for separate status updates.

constraints.title: Use spec and status for varying state
constraints.rule: Objects whose actual state may vary from user intent have both `spec` and `status`.
constraints.rationale: This captures the main spec/status structuring rule.

constraints.title: No extra top-level fields beside spec/status and metadata
constraints.rule: Objects that contain both `spec` and `status` do not add other top-level fields beyond standard metadata.
constraints.rationale: This is a local structural constraint on top-level shape.

constraints.title: Conditions complement detailed status
constraints.rule: Conditions complement more detailed status information rather than replacing it.
constraints.rationale: This is a semantic design constraint for status modeling.

constraints.title: Treat conditions as keyed map
constraints.rule: Condition collections are treated as maps keyed by `type`.
constraints.rationale: This maps directly to the documented conditions shape.

constraints.title: Follow standard conditions schema
constraints.rule: Conditions follow the standard `metav1.Condition` schema.
constraints.rationale: This is explicit but usually checked from the surrounding type definition.

constraints.title: Put conditions at top level of status
constraints.rule: Conditions are included as a top-level element in `status`.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct structural rule.

constraints.title: Require Reason field
constraints.rule: Condition entries include the `Reason` field.
constraints.rationale: This is an explicit required-field rule.

constraints.title: Use PascalCase condition types
constraints.rule: Condition type names use PascalCase.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct naming rule.

constraints.title: Prefer conditions over phase
constraints.rule: Newer API types use conditions instead of `phase`.
constraints.rationale: These two statements together express the intended migration away from `phase`.

constraints.title: Use int32 or int64 for public integers
constraints.rule: Public integer fields use Go `int32` or `int64`, not `int`.
constraints.rationale: This is a precise type-level rule.

constraints.title: Serialize oversized integers as strings
constraints.rule: Numeric fields that can exceed JSON-safe integer precision are serialized and accepted as strings.
constraints.rationale: This rule depends on the expected numeric range of the field.

constraints.title: Use Kubernetes constant style
constraints.rule: APIs within Kubernetes use the documented constant naming style.
constraints.rationale: This is a project-wide naming/style constraint.

constraints.title: Use CamelCase for new flag values
constraints.rule: New flag values use CamelCase only.
constraints.rationale: This is a local naming rule for new flag constants.

constraints.title: Mark every field optional or required
constraints.rule: Every field is marked as either optional or required.
constraints.rationale: This is an explicit completeness rule for field requiredness.

constraints.title: Tag new fields explicitly
constraints.rule: New fields explicitly set either `+optional` or `+required`.
constraints.rationale: This is the concrete tagging rule for new fields.

constraints.title: Reject unset required fields
constraints.rule: The API server does not allow POST or PUT requests with required fields unset.
constraints.rationale: This is explicit but manifests via validation behavior.

constraints.title: Use pointers for bool fields
constraints.rule: `bool` fields use pointer types.
constraints.rationale: This is one half of the bool-field serialization rule.

constraints.title: Use omitempty for bool fields
constraints.rule: `bool` fields use the `omitempty` tag.
constraints.rationale: This is the other half of the bool-field serialization rule.

constraints.title: Static defaults depend only on the object
constraints.rule: Static defaulting considers only the object being operated upon.
constraints.rationale: This is a determinism rule for static defaults.

constraints.title: Admission-defaulted fields are optional
constraints.rule: Fields initialized by admission-controlled defaults are strictly optional.
constraints.rationale: This is an explicit requiredness consequence of late/default initialization.

constraints.title: Controller-defaulted fields are optional
constraints.rule: Fields initialized by controllers are strictly optional.
constraints.rationale: This is the same late-initialization rule for controller-assigned defaults.

constraints.title: Defaulting only sets previously unset fields
constraints.rule: Defaulting only sets previously unset fields.
constraints.rationale: This is one atomic piece of the additive-only defaulting rule.

constraints.title: Defaulting only adds map keys
constraints.rule: Defaulting only adds keys to maps.
constraints.rationale: This is one atomic piece of the additive-only defaulting rule.

constraints.title: Defaulting only adds mergeable array values
constraints.rule: Defaulting only adds values to arrays with mergeable semantics.
constraints.rationale: This is one atomic piece of the additive-only defaulting rule.

constraints.title: Serialize dates as RFC3339 strings
constraints.rule: Dates are serialized as RFC3339 strings.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct serialization rule.

constraints.title: Represent durations as integer+unit fields
constraints.rule: Duration fields are represented as integer fields with units in the field name.
constraints.rationale: This is an explicit field-shape rule.

constraints.title: Keep namespaced references in-namespace
constraints.rule: Object references on a namespaced type usually refer only to objects in the same namespace.
constraints.rationale: This is a semantic API-boundary rule.

constraints.title: Name reference fields with Ref suffix
constraints.rule: Reference field names use the `{field}Ref` format.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct naming rule for object references.

constraints.title: Use single-value fieldPath
constraints.rule: `fieldPath` points to a single value and uses field selector notation.
constraints.rationale: This is explicit but requires inspecting the chosen field path syntax.

constraints.title: Prefer HTTP header semantics over duplicate status fields
constraints.rule: When a status field duplicates a standard HTTP header meaning and that header is returned, the HTTP header takes priority.
constraints.rationale: This is a semantic precedence rule for response interpretation.

constraints.title: Generate user-relevant events
constraints.rule: Generate events for situations users or administrators should be alerted about.
constraints.rationale: This is guidance on when events should be emitted.

constraints.title: Use PascalCase for Go field names
constraints.rule: Go field names use PascalCase.
constraints.rationale: This is one atomic piece of the documented field naming rule.

constraints.title: Use camelCase for JSON field names
constraints.rule: JSON field names use camelCase.
constraints.rationale: This is one atomic piece of the documented field naming rule.

constraints.title: Avoid underscores and dashes in field names
constraints.rule: Go and JSON field names do not contain underscores or dashes.
constraints.rationale: This is one atomic piece of the documented field naming rule.

constraints.title: Match Go and JSON field names
constraints.rule: Go and JSON field names almost always match except for initial capitalization.
constraints.rationale: This is the cross-representation consistency part of the naming rule.

constraints.title: Use declarative field and resource names
constraints.rule: Field and resource names are declarative rather than imperative.
constraints.rationale: This is a semantic naming rule.

constraints.title: Avoid FooController kind names
constraints.rule: Kinds do not use the deprecated `FooController` naming pattern.
constraints.rationale: This is a deprecated naming convention rule.

constraints.title: Use somethingTime suffix
constraints.rule: Fields representing the time when something occurs are named `somethingTime`, not `stamp`.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct field naming rule.

constraints.title: Use lowercase dashed map keys
constraints.rule: Label and annotation key names are all lowercase with words separated by dashes.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct key-format rule.

constraints.title: Prefix non-user labels and annotations
constraints.rule: Labels and annotations other than end-user keys are prefixed.
constraints.rationale: This is an explicit prefixing rule.

constraints.title: Prefer kubernetes.io for new keys
constraints.rule: New label and annotation map keys use the `kubernetes.io` form rather than `k8s.io`.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct namespace/prefix preference for new keys.

constraints.title: Use context-rich key prefixes
constraints.rule: Label and annotation key prefixes carry as much context as possible.
constraints.rationale: This is a semantic naming-quality rule.

constraints.title: Prefer declarative validation
constraints.rule: New APIs prefer declarative validation where it supports the rule.
constraints.rationale: This is the headline validation-design recommendation.

constraints.title: Validate requiredness explicitly
constraints.rule: Fields are declared optional or required, and validation checks that requiredness.
constraints.rationale: This combines declaration and validation of requiredness into one local rule.

constraints.title: Validate string formats
constraints.rule: Almost all string fields are checked for format.
constraints.rationale: This is a broad but local validation expectation.

constraints.title: Validate string maximum length
constraints.rule: String fields are checked for maximum length.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct validation rule.

constraints.title: Bounds-check numeric fields
constraints.rule: Numeric fields are bounds-checked for both minimum and maximum values.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct validation rule.

constraints.title: Validate list maximum size
constraints.rule: List fields are checked for maximum size.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct validation rule.

constraints.title: Set listType tags
constraints.rule: List fields have their `listType` tag set.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct structural tagging rule.

constraints.title: Validate uniqueness for set/map lists
constraints.rule: Lists with set or map semantics are checked for uniqueness.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct validation rule for list semantics.

constraints.title: Validate map maximum size
constraints.rule: Map fields are checked for maximum size.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct validation rule.

constraints.title: Validate map keys
constraints.rule: Map fields validate their keys as well as their values.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct validation rule.

constraints.title: Use must for positive requirements
constraints.rule: Positive validation requirements use the word `must`.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct error-message wording rule.

constraints.title: Use must not for negative formatting requirements
constraints.rule: Negative formatting requirements use the phrase `must not`.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct error-message wording rule.

constraints.title: Use may not for negative behavioral requirements
constraints.rule: Negative behavioral requirements use the phrase `may not`.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct error-message wording rule.

constraints.title: Quote literal strings with single quotes
constraints.rule: Literal string values in validation messages are written in single quotes.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct error-message formatting rule.

constraints.title: Quote field names with back-quotes
constraints.rule: Referenced field names in validation messages are written in back-quotes.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct error-message formatting rule.

constraints.title: Use words for inequalities
constraints.rule: Validation messages express inequalities with words rather than symbols.
constraints.rationale: This is a direct error-message wording rule.

constraints.title: Verify user-requested allocated values
constraints.rule: The system does not trust user-specified allocated values and verifies or confirms them before use.
constraints.rationale: This is the central safety rule for allocated-value fields.

constraints.title: Prefer status for new APIs in this pattern
constraints.rule: New APIs use `status` for this pattern.
constraints.rationale: This is the explicit design recommendation in the allocated-values discussion.

constraints.title: Controllers consider async ordering
constraints.rule: Controller implementations take care around ordering of asynchronous operations.
constraints.rationale: This is guidance on controller behavior around status updates.

constraints.title: Actuate before status when necessary
constraints.rule: If exposing `status` before actuation would be problematic, controllers actuate first and update `status` afterward.
constraints.rationale: This is an explicit ordering rule for status updates.

constraints.title: Handle interrupted control loops idempotently
constraints.rule: Controllers handle interrupted control loops in an idempotent and consistent way.
constraints.rationale: This is the main resilience rule for status-handling controllers.

constraints.title: Account for allocated object lifecycle and linkage
constraints.rule: When using this pattern, APIs account for the lifecycle and linkage of allocated objects.
constraints.rationale: This is the explicit cleanup/linkage rule for separately allocated objects.
